reason: Frontend layer entry — routing helper, AppImpl entry-point wiring, LeftSidebar UI hide rules, ClaudeSessions panel filter all live here.
workspaceMode is the routing gate; single source — no new repositoryIds.length / rootPath===repo.path implicit branches; activeProjectId/activeRepositoryId ownership is App-shell coordination.
useWorkspaceMode is the canonical consumer; new routing helper stays pure (no side effects); entry-point wiring uses useCallback with correct dep arrays.
`resolveSidebarSelectionTarget` returns a discriminated union {kind:'project-main'|'per-repo'}; consumers must exhaustively narrow. ClaudeSessions filter must stay type-safe at boundary.
LeftSidebar must not call invoke directly; RepositoryConversationAction hide rule driven by workspaceMode prop, not component-side IPC.
§3 employee-UI hide-rule precedent — analogous pattern for repo-row chat icon hide; verify shouldHideEmployeeUi semantics don't regress when multi_repo behavior changes.
Routing helper under src/utils/ (pure); session creation orchestration stays in AppImpl; no new src/pages/.
No new IPC expected — confirm bindRepositoryMainSession reuse is sufficient and no new command name is needed.
No new migration — confirm schema-neutral promise (per-repo legacy session rows untouched, only UI-filtered).
Single direction: useWorkspaceMode → resolveSidebarSelectionTarget → AppImpl orchestration → services/* → invoke. No reverse.

Reuse `resolveProjectMainSessionAnchor`, `bindRepositoryMainSession`, `pickSessionForRepositorySidebarSelect`, `resolveBoundMainSessionId`. Do NOT duplicate anchor logic or invent a new session-binding store.
